component.name: jakarta.enterprise.cdi-api
component.description: APIs for CDI (Contexts and Dependency Injection for Java)

component.name: jakarta.enterprise.lang-model
component.description: Build Compatible (Reflection-Free) Java Language Model for CDI

component.name: jakarta.el-api
component.description: Jakarta Expression Language defines an expression language for Java applications

license.name: GNU General Public License, version 2 with the GNU Classpath Exception

component.name: jakarta.interceptor-api
component.description: Jakarta Interceptors defines a means of interposing on business method invocations and specific events—such as lifecycle events and timeout events—that occur on instances of Jakarta EE components and other managed classes.

component.name: mutiny
component.description: Intuitive Event-Driven Reactive Programming Library for Java

component.name: quarkus-ide-launcher
component.description: Build parent to bring in required dependencies

component.name: quarkus-development-mode-spi
component.description: SPI classes for Quarkus Development mode.

component.name: jakarta.json-api
component.description: Jakarta JSON Processing defines a Java(R) based framework for parsing, generating, transforming, and querying JSON documents.

license.name: GNU General Public License, version 2 with the GNU Classpath Exception

component.name: jboss-threads
component.description: Thread pool implementations and threading utilities

component.name: slf4j-jboss-logmanager
component.description: An adaptor for SLF4J which delegates to the JBoss Log Manager

component.name: wildfly-common
component.description: Provides, via submodules, a base configuration for JBoss project builds, as well as a derived configuration supporting multi-release JARs

component.name: quarkus-value-registry
component.description: The API to store runtime values. It has its own module, so it can be shared between unrelated modules, and it is a parent first dependency.

component.name: quarkus-bootstrap-runner
component.description: The entry point for production applications using the custom ClassLoader. This contains the production ClassLoader code and must not have any non parent first dependencies.

component.name: quarkus-rest
component.description: A Jakarta REST implementation utilizing build time processing and Vert.x. This extension is not compatible with the quarkus-resteasy extension, or any of the extensions that depend on it.

component.name: quarkus-rest-common
component.description: Common runtime parts of Quarkus REST

component.name: reactive-streams
component.description: A Protocol for Asynchronous Non-Blocking Data Sequence

component.name: mutiny-zero-flow-adapters
component.description: Adapters from/to JDK Flow and Reactive Streams

component.name: quarkus-mutiny
component.description: Write reactive applications with the modern Reactive Programming library Mutiny

component.name: quarkus-smallrye-context-propagation
component.description: Propagate contexts between managed threads in reactive applications

component.name: smallrye-context-propagation
component.description: A pluggable library for context propagation in reactive libraries

component.name: smallrye-context-propagation-api
component.description: A pluggable library for context propagation in reactive libraries

component.name: smallrye-context-propagation-storage
component.description: A pluggable library for context propagation in reactive libraries

component.name: mutiny-smallrye-context-propagation
component.description: Intuitive Event-Driven Reactive Programming Library for Java

component.name: quarkus-vertx
component.description: Write reactive applications with the Vert.x API

component.name: quarkus-netty
component.description: Netty is a non-blocking I/O client-server framework. Used by Quarkus as foundation layer.

component.name: netty-codec
component.description: Netty is an asynchronous event-driven network application framework for rapid development of maintainable high performance protocol servers and clients.

component.name: netty-codec-haproxy
component.description: Netty is an asynchronous event-driven network application framework for rapid development of maintainable high performance protocol servers and clients.

component.name: quarkus-vertx-latebound-mdc-provider
component.description: Quarkus Vert.x Late Bound MDC Provider

component.name: quarkus-vertx-utils
component.description: Ancillary classes for making third party frameworks to run on top of Vert.x

component.name: commons-logging-jboss-logging
component.description: An Apache Commons Logging implementation which logs through a JBoss Logging logger.

component.name: quarkus-security-runtime-spi
component.description: Build parent to bring in required dependencies

component.name: quarkus-tls-registry-spi
component.description: Build parent to bring in required dependencies

component.name: smallrye-private-key-pem-parser
component.description: An API and Maven plugin to generate TLS certificates

component.name: quarkus-credentials
component.description: Build parent to bring in required dependencies

component.name: smallrye-common-vertx-context
component.description: A set of utility classes to ease the usage of Vert.x 4 context locals and duplicated contexts.

component.name: quarkus-security
component.description: Provides, via submodules, a base configuration for JBoss project builds, as well as a derived configuration supporting multi-release JARs

component.name: crac
component.description: A wrapper for OpenJDK CRaC API to build and run on any JDK

component.name: brotli4j
component.description: Brotli4j provides Brotli compression and decompression for Java.

component.name: service
component.description: Brotli4j provides Brotli compression and decompression for Java.

component.name: native-osx-x86_64
component.description: Brotli4j provides Brotli compression and decompression for Java.

license.name: GNU General Public License, version 2 with the GNU Classpath Exception

component.description: Netty is an asynchronous event-driven network application framework for rapid development of maintainable high performance protocol servers and clients.
